LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT CHARLES SCHWAB & CO. INC'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT WAS TAKEN UNDER SUBMISSION. JUDGE: LORETTA M. GIORGI, REPORTER: KARLA ELLIS-DAVIS, CSR #12998 ---------------------------------------------------- AFTER HEARING, THE COURT RULED AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER TO THE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED. PLAINTIFF HAS ADEQUATELY PLED FACTS THAT SHOW THAT CORCORAN WAS CLOTHED WITH APPARENT AUTHORITY TO MAKE REPRESENTATION ABOUT PRODUCTS CARRIED ON THE DEFENDANT SCHWAB'S PLATFORM, INCLUDING TRANSTAR. WHETHER OR NOT THIS IS ENOUGH FOR A REASONABLE PERSON TO BELIEVE THAT THERE WAS AN AGENCY RELATIONSHIP IS A QUESTION OF FACT THAT IS NOT APPROPRIATE FOR DETERMINATION ON A DEMURRER. PARTIES NOTIFIED OF THE COURT'S RULING BY TELEPHONE. PLAINTIFF IS DIRECTED TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER.

MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT CHARLES SCHWAB & CO. INC'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT.
Appearances: Simona A. Agnolucci and Gilbert R. Serota of Howard Rice (677.6306) for defendant and moving party; Dawn Schock of Albert & Will (310.257.9363) appearing via Court Call for plaintiff and responding party; Jeremy Scott Carr of Winget Spadafora & Schwartzberg LLP (310.836.4800) for defendant Wayne Corcoran.
Argued and the court took the matter under submission. (302/LMG) Judge: Loretta M. Giorgi, Reporter: Karla Ellis-Davis, CSR #12998
---
After hearing, the court ruled as follows: Defendant's demurrer to the 2nd amended complaint is overruled. Plaintiff has adequately pled facts that show that Corcoran was clothed with apparent authority to make representation about products carried on the defendant Schwab's platform, including Transtar. Whether or not this is enough for a reasonable person to believe that there was an agency relationship is a question of fact that is not appropriate for determination on a demurrer.
Parties notified of the court's ruling by telephone. Plaintiff is directed to submit a proposed form of order.
